metadata.title: Reference corpus of the Raëlian canon
metadata.description: Structured catalogue of every text, tradition, figure, language, and material site that the three canonical Raëlian books reference, either directly (named, quoted, or cited with chapter-and-verse) or indirectly (alluded to, named in passing, or engaged through etymological reading or motif).
scope: The Book Which Tells The Truth (Le Livre Qui Dit La Vérité, 1974)
Extra-Terrestrials Took Me To Their Planet (Les Extra-Terrestres M'ont Emmené Sur Leur Planète, 1975)
Let's Welcome The Extra-Terrestrials (Accueillir les Extra-Terrestres, 1979)
metadata.base_text: Intelligent Design — Message From the Designers, English omnibus edition, 2005 (combining all three canonical books)

tier_definitions.direct: Text, figure, or tradition the canon names explicitly, quotes from, or attributes a numbered chapter-and-verse to.
tier_definitions.indirect: Text, tradition, language, figure, or motif the canon engages without formal citation — through paraphrase, etymological reading, name-checking in lists, or allusion to characteristic content.

hebrew_bible.name: Genesis
hebrew_bible.canonical_locus: TBWTT — chapters 'Genesis', 'The Flood', 'The Tower of Babel', 'Sodom and Gomorrah', 'The Sacrifice of Abraham', 'Moses'
hebrew_bible.reading: Re-read as a compressed historical record of the Elohim creation project, the Flood, the dispersion at Babel, the nuclear destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah, and early covenantal contact.
hebrew_bible.notes: The densest single locus of citation in the entire canon.

hebrew_bible.name: Exodus
hebrew_bible.reading: The burning bush, pillar of cloud and fire, parting of the sea, manna, and Sinai theophany are read as technological encounters with the Elohim.

hebrew_bible.name: Joshua
hebrew_bible.reading: The fall of Jericho is read as a sonic-weapon episode.

hebrew_bible.name: Judges
hebrew_bible.reading: Samson's strength is reinterpreted as a telepathic capability.

hebrew_bible.name: 1–2 Kings
hebrew_bible.reading: The chariot of fire is read as a spacecraft.

hebrew_bible.name: Job
hebrew_bible.canonical_locus: Quoted in passing; flagged in editorial notes for Dhorme/RNKJV divergence
hebrew_bible.reading: Specific verses noted as preserving information about Elohim technology in the Dhorme translation that English versions obscure.

hebrew_bible.name: Psalms
hebrew_bible.reading: Engaged as poetic record of the Elohim relationship to the people of Israel.

hebrew_bible.name: Ezekiel
hebrew_bible.canonical_locus: TBWTT — chapter 'The Flying Saucers of Ezekiel'; ETTMTTP — chapter 'A Foretaste of Paradise'
hebrew_bible.reading: Ezekiel 1 is read as a detailed eyewitness description of an Elohim landing; Ezekiel 37 (the valley of dry bones) is read as a description of the biological-replication machine.

hebrew_bible.name: Song of Songs (Canticle of Canticles)
books_or_loci: Canticle 5 — within the Kabbalistic / Shi'ur Qomah reading
hebrew_bible.canonical_locus: TBWTT — chapter 'At the Root of All Religions'
hebrew_bible.reading: The canon attributes to a Kabbalistic reading of the Canticle a cosmological passage measuring the 'height of the creator' in parasangs.
hebrew_bible.notes: The reference is mediated through the Kabbalah; the canon names both.

new_testament.name: Gospel of Matthew
new_testament.reading: The most extensively quoted single book of the New Testament. The Parable of the Sower (Matthew 13) is given a specific reading as a description of life seeded on multiple worlds.

new_testament.name: Gospel of Mark
new_testament.reading: Mark 16:19 (Ascension) and Mark 16:18 ('they shall take up serpents') quoted.

new_testament.name: Gospel of Luke
new_testament.reading: Luke 21:29-30 (parable of the fig tree) quoted as a sign of apocalyptic timing.

new_testament.name: Acts of the Apostles
new_testament.reading: Pentecost (Acts 2) and Peter's prison release (Acts 12) are read as further Elohim interventions; Acts 17:28 ('we are also his offspring') given particular weight.

new_testament.name: Revelation / Apocalypse of John
new_testament.canonical_locus: LWTE — 'The New Revelations: The Devil Does Not Exist, I Met Him' and 'The Apocalypse of the Final Nuclear Cataclysm'
new_testament.reading: Treated as a dictation given to John through a visual-display apparatus. The seven 'golden lampstands' are read as seven craft; the four horsemen are read in nuclear-eschatological register.

bible_translations_named.name: Édouard Dhorme — La Bible (Bibliothèque de la Pléiade)
bible_translations_named.canonical_locus: Named in the omnibus's editorial Notes & References as Rael's working text
bible_translations_named.reading: The Dhorme rendering of Hebrew vocabulary (notably 'science' for what other translations render 'good and evil') is treated as preserving meaning.

bible_translations_named.name: Restored Name King James Version
bible_translations_named.canonical_locus: Named in the omnibus's Notes & References as the default English version used throughout
bible_translations_named.reading: Chosen for its restoration of Elohim and Yahweh in place of God and the Lord.

bible_translations_named.name: New King James Version
bible_translations_named.canonical_locus: Named in the omnibus's Notes & References
bible_translations_named.reading: Used selectively where the RNKJV diverges from the canon's preferred reading.

bible_translations_named.name: American Standard Version (1901)
bible_translations_named.canonical_locus: Named in the omnibus's Notes & References

bible_translations_named.name: Webster Bible (1833)
bible_translations_named.canonical_locus: Named in the omnibus's Notes & References
bible_translations_named.reading: Selected for the rendering 'on the earth' rather than 'in the earth' in specific passages.

bible_translations_named.name: Hebrew Names Version (of the World English Bible)
bible_translations_named.canonical_locus: Named in the omnibus's Notes & References

bible_translations_named.name: Darby Translation
bible_translations_named.canonical_locus: Named in the omnibus's Notes & References
bible_translations_named.reading: Selected for its rendering 'nations' (rather than 'Gentiles') in specific passages.

bible_translations_named.name: Latin Vulgate (Jerome, ca. 405 CE)
bible_translations_named.canonical_locus: Cited in the omnibus's Notes & References
bible_translations_named.reading: Invoked twice to settle questions of meaning (caelis, desertum).

other_named_texts.name: The Kabbalah
other_named_texts.canonical_locus: TBWTT — chapter 'At the Root of All Religions'
other_named_texts.reading: Named as 'the closest book to the truth.' A specific passage measuring the 'height of the creator' in parasangs is attributed to it. The passage description matches motifs from the Shi'ur Qomah tradition.
other_named_texts.notes: The canon names 'the Kabbalah' generically rather than identifying a specific text (Zohar, Bahir, Shi'ur Qomah).

other_named_texts.name: Qur'an
other_named_texts.canonical_locus: ETTMTTP — 'To the People of Israel' and ETTMTTP — Yahweh's address to the prophets
other_named_texts.reading: Quoted to establish that Muhammad himself acknowledged the prior Hebrew and Christian prophetic line.

other_named_texts.name: The Book of Enoch (1 Enoch)
other_named_texts.reading: Named in the list of texts containing traces of the Elohim story.
other_named_texts.notes: The reference is from a follower's commentary preserved in the canonical omnibus, not from Rael directly.

named_messengers.name: Moses
named_messengers.reading: First-listed Elohim messenger; recipient of Sinai contact; presented as resident on the planet of the Eternals.

named_messengers.name: Elijah
named_messengers.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'Elijah the Messenger'; ETTMTTP — 'Meeting the Ancient Prophets'
named_messengers.reading: Taken up bodily in 2 Kings 2; resident on the planet of the Eternals.

named_messengers.name: Jesus
named_messengers.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'The Role of Christ'; ETTMTTP — 'Meeting the Ancient Prophets'
named_messengers.reading: Sent specifically to spread the Hebrew-prophetic message worldwide; recreated from a preserved cell.

named_messengers.name: Buddha (Siddhārtha Gautama)
named_messengers.reading: Treated as a major Elohim messenger to East and South Asia.

named_messengers.name: Muhammad
named_messengers.canonical_locus: ETTMTTP — 'Meeting the Ancient Prophets'; LWTE — addendum
named_messengers.reading: Within whose writings, the canon states, the Elohim leader was named Allah 'out of respect.'

named_messengers.name: Joseph Smith
named_messengers.reading: Named directly as a prophet of the same line as Moses, Jesus, Buddha, and Muhammad.

named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.name: Judaism
named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.canonical_locus: ETTMTTP — 'To the People of Israel'; TBWTT — 'The Creation of the State of Israel'
named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.reading: Engaged directly as the originating recipient tradition; the State of Israel is given a specific eschatological role.

named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.name: Christianity (Roman Catholic and Protestant)
named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'The End of the Church'; LWTE — 'An Atheist Religion'
named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.reading: Critiqued at length, especially the Roman Catholic institution.

named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.name: Buddhism
named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.reading: Engaged at length; called 'the religion closest to the Truth' in the 1997 addendum.

named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.name: Latter-day Saints / Mormonism
named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'At the Root of All Religions'; LWTE — 'An Atheist Religion'
named_traditions_engaged_doctrinally.reading: Named directly; Joseph Smith placed in the prophetic line.

traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.name: Greek mythology
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'At the Root of All Religions'; LWTE — 'Yes... I am Raelian'
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.reading: Named as containing 'important testimonies,' tied geographically to alleged Elohim bases in Greece. No specific Greek myth is quoted.

traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.name: Mahābhārata (with the Vedas and Rāmāyaṇa)
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.reading: Listed as a 'mythical epic poem of India' carrying traces. No specific verses cited.
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.notes: The list pairs the Vedas and the Rāmāyaṇa as components of the Mahābhārata, which is philologically inaccurate but reflects the canon's own framing.

traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.name: Kojiki
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.reading: Listed as reporting 'that which happened at the beginning.' No specific passage cited.

traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.name: Popol Vuh
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.reading: Listed as a Latin American testimony. No specific passage cited.

traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.name: Chronicle of Akakor
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.tradition: Purportedly indigenous Amazonian; widely treated as a 20th-century literary construction by Karl Brugger (1976)
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.reading: Listed as a Latin American testimony. No specific passage cited.
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.notes: Source tension: the Chronicle of Akakor's authenticity is contested; its appearance in the list is treated by Wheel of Heaven as an open question.

traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.name: Epic of Gilgamesh
traditions_named_as_carrying_traces.reading: Listed as a Mesopotamian testimony. No specific tablet or passage cited.

languages_engaged_etymologically.name: Biblical Hebrew
key_terms: Elohim ('those who came from the sky')

material_and_geographic_traces.name: The Andes / Peru
material_and_geographic_traces.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'At the Root of All Religions'; LWTE — 'Yes... I am Raelian'
material_and_geographic_traces.reading: Region of alleged Elohim base activity.

material_and_geographic_traces.name: The Himalayas / Tibet
material_and_geographic_traces.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'At the Root of All Religions'; LWTE — 'Yes... I am Raelian'
material_and_geographic_traces.reading: Region of alleged Elohim base activity.

material_and_geographic_traces.name: Greece
material_and_geographic_traces.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'At the Root of All Religions'
material_and_geographic_traces.reading: Region of alleged Elohim base activity, correlated with the Greek mythological tradition.

material_and_geographic_traces.name: Nazca Lines
material_and_geographic_traces.reading: Cited as 'engravings on the plain of Nazca' — a material trace.

material_and_geographic_traces.name: Easter Island (Rapa Nui / Île de Pâques)
Atlantis and Mu (lost-continent legends)
material_and_geographic_traces.reading: Engaged not as a source but as legends the canon explains: distorted memories of the antediluvian single continent broken up by the Elohim.

motifs_engaged_without_citation.name: Universal flood narratives
motifs_engaged_without_citation.reading: The reading of Noah's ark as a spacecraft / genetic-preservation vessel underlies the wider family of flood traditions.

motifs_engaged_without_citation.name: Confusion of tongues / language-origin narratives
motifs_engaged_without_citation.reading: Read as a specific historical episode of forced dispersion.

motifs_engaged_without_citation.name: Chariot-of-fire / sky-ascent narratives
motifs_engaged_without_citation.canonical_locus: TBWTT — 'Elijah the Messenger', 'The Flying Saucers of Ezekiel'
motifs_engaged_without_citation.reading: Elijah and Ezekiel as paradigmatic instances of a broader family of sky-vehicle traditions.

motifs_engaged_without_citation.name: Council-of-gods / bnei-elohim narratives
motifs_engaged_without_citation.reading: The 'sons of Elohim and daughters of men' passage engaged through the plural reading of Elohim.

source_tensions.name: Dhorme / RNKJV translation divergence
source_tensions.description: The French canon and the English omnibus do not always quote the same biblical wording. The editorial notes acknowledge this. Some Wheel of Heaven readings are sensitive to which translation a reader has in hand.

source_tensions.name: Status of the 'Yes... I am Raelian' enumeration
source_tensions.description: The list of Kojiki, Popol Vuh, Akakor, Gilgamesh, and the Mahābhārata appears in a commentator's contribution to Let's Welcome The Extra-Terrestrials, not in Rael's own voice. Its weight relative to the primary tier is an open editorial question.

source_tensions.name: Generic 'Kabbalah' vs. specific Shi'ur Qomah
source_tensions.description: The parasangs / 'height of the creator' passage attributed to the Kabbalah matches motifs from the Shi'ur Qomah tradition rather than the Zohar proper. Whether to gloss the canon's 'Kabbalah' as Shi'ur Qomah is unresolved.

source_tensions.name: The Chronicle of Akakor problem
source_tensions.description: The Chronicle of Akakor, published in 1976 by Karl Brugger, is widely treated as a literary hoax. Its appearance in the canon's traces list — published only a few years after Brugger's book — sits awkwardly with the rest of the catalogue, which is composed of texts with deep traditional pedigrees.

source_tensions.name: Direct vs. indirect borderline cases
source_tensions.description: Some entries are borderline. Joseph Smith is named directly but no Mormon scripture is quoted, so the Mormon tradition sits in the secondary tier even though the founder is primary. Conversely, the Kabbalah is named generically (secondary-tier language) but a specific cosmological passage is engaged (primary-tier behaviour).
